setting.note: Isegawa's Uganda runs from a Catholic childhood in the Buganda countryside south of the capital, through the boarding seminary, and into Kampala itself: the seven hills, the markets and the slums, and the fear of the Amin and Obote years. The map holds to the real Ugandan geography the novel walks, before its narrator finally leaves for the Netherlands.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Moses Isegawa, first published in Dutch as Abessijnse kronieken in 1998 and in English in 2000. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Abyssinian Chronicles, Moses Isegawa (2000). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of the Buganda hills and banana groves the narrator grows up among. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
